fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 61 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective_expiry`), so when `now` exactly equals the TTL boundary the entry is incorrectly served instead of being treated as expired.

constraints.intent: At the boundary where now equals effective_expiry, the condition should be true (entry expired), but the strict > makes it false, causing the bug.
After computing effective_expiry, verify that when now equals effective_expiry the subsequent branch will correctly return None (i.e., the >= condition is needed).
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than effective_expiry; if now == effective_expiry we should have returned None but didn't.
Before using effective_expiry in the comparison, the boundary case (now == effective_expiry) should be treated as expired, which the current strict > fails to do.
